frame_analyses.description: Title card showing 'Coordination' simulation procedure with instructional text about calibration and exercise setup
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational content with step-by-step setup instructions for LapSim trainer

frame_analyses.description: Detailed instructions displayed covering gallstone pickup, target bag placement, and repetition cycles
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Written instructions explaining that gallstones disappear when fully within target, time pressure element mentioned

frame_analyses.description: LapSim trainer interface loading screen showing 'LapSim by surgicalscience' branding
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Software interface preparation before performance begins

frame_analyses.description: Laparoscopic view of simulated abdominal cavity with textured tissue surface. Instruction: 'Find the gallstone and touch it with the instrument'
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clear view of simulated anatomy with gallstone visible as small gold-colored object. Single laparoscopic instrument visible entering field

frame_analyses.description: Instrument tip approaching gallstone from superior aspect. Gallstone clearly visible and stationary
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good visualization. Instrument moving deliberately toward target with controlled motion

frame_analyses.description: Instrument grasping gallstone. Gallstone remains in view and appears grasped
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean pickup motion. Instrument orientation appropriate for grasp

frame_analyses.description: Gallstone being transported upward and rightward toward target bag visible at upper right of field
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth transport movement. Target bag location clearly indicated by mesh structure

frame_analyses.description: Gallstone being lowered into target bag. Instruction updated: 'Move the gallstone into the target bag'
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Controlled placement. Gallstone positioned for entry into target

frame_analyses.description: Gallstone fully within target bag - object begins to disappear/dissolve confirming successful placement
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Successful transfer indicated by gallstone disappearance. Instruction counter updated to '(# gallstones left: 4)'

frame_analyses.description: Next gallstone located in lower left field. Instruction: 'Find the next gallstone and touch it with the instrument tip'. Counter shows 4 remaining
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good field visualization for locating subsequent gallstones. Trainee efficiently searching field

frame_analyses.description: Results screen displayed showing performance metrics. Task completed with Status: 'You Passed!' and Score: 81%
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Automated performance evaluation shown. Total Time: 38.88 seconds. Multiple performance parameters displayed with color-coded scoring (red/yellow/green gradient)

gallstone_transfers.note: Video ends with results screen showing 4 gallstones transferred (counter shows 1 remaining at 38.88s mark)

score_components.note: This is an INSTRUCTIONAL video, not a performance to be scored under FLS official rubric. The embedded results screen shows LapSim software scores, which differ from official MISTELS FLS scoring. Official FLS peg transfer scoring would be: 300 - 38.88 - (penalties for any drops/failures) = approximately 261 points if no penalties, placing this in the EXPERT range (40-60s is expert benchmark).

confidence_rationale: This is clearly an instructional/educational video showing LapSim training software interface with embedded automated scoring. The video includes setup instructions, tutorial text, and an automated performance evaluation. While the video contains a simulated performance, it is presented as an instructional demonstration by the LapSim platform itself, not a raw trainee performance. Completion appears interrupted (video ends during transfer 5 of 6), and scoring is from LapSim's proprietary algorithm, not FLS official rubric.
cannot_determine: Official FLS MISTELS score — video uses LapSim proprietary metrics rather than FLS rubric
Whether performance is trainee or instructional demo — likely instructional given interface presentation
Exact reason for task incompleteness (timeout vs. intentional demo end)
Penalty breakdown under official FLS scoring — LapSim reports tissue damage (3 events, 0.25mm max) but this differs from FLS penalty structure
video_classification_rationale: This video is classified as 'instructional' because: (1) it begins with titled setup procedures and written instructional text; (2) it displays the LapSim trainer interface with overlay instructions ('Find gallstone...', 'Move into target bag'); (3) it ends with an automated results screen showing LapSim scoring, not raw video of a standalone performance; (4) the presentation includes educational framing (Didactics, Instructions, Results tabs visible) rather than simple performance capture.

demonstrated_techniques: Gallstone pickup using controlled grasper approach
Smooth midair transport of objects between pegs
Placement of object into target container with precision
Field awareness and efficiency in locating sequential objects
Efficient camera control to track instrument and target
verbal_cues: Only have a few seconds before gallstone times out and disappears
Continue to move gallstones until exercise completed
common_errors_addressed: Inadequate instrument calibration before task start
Poor camera following leading to loss of field visualization
Hesitation or slow movement causing gallstone timeout/disappearance
Inefficient search patterns for locating multiple objects

improvement_suggestions: Understand that LapSim scoring (81%) differs from official FLS MISTELS rubric — official training should use validated FLS scoring
If performing for official FLS certification, maintain completion of all 6 transfers within 300 second maximum
Review tissue damage events (3 recorded) to identify unnecessary collisions or aggressive movements
Optimize camera control to reduce outside-field moments (1 recorded) for improved visualization
note_on_scoring: This video does NOT receive an official FLS score because it is instructional content with embedded LapSim software results, not a standalone performance video suitable for FLS proctor evaluation. If a trainee's raw performance were submitted for official FLS MISTELS scoring on the Peg Transfer task, the scoring formula would be: Score = 300 − completion_time − penalties (drops, failures, inefficiencies). The hypothetical official FLS score for this embedded performance (assuming all 6 transfers completed with only the 3 tissue damage events as minor penalties) would approximate 255–265 points, indicating expert-level speed.
